LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS MARKEL AMERICAN INSURANCE COMPANY AND MARKEL INSURANCE COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO THE SECOND, THIRD AND FIFTH CAUSES OF ACTION IN THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFF JERILYNNE AKUTAGAWA IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR NEGLIGENT MISREPRESENTATION AND OVERRULED AS TO THE THIRD AND FIFTH CAUSES OF ACTION FOR FRAUD. MS. AKUTAGAWA ALLEGES THAT DEFENDANTS NEGLIGENTLY MISREPRESENTED THAT THEY WOULD PAY THE $125,000. (FAC PAR. 36 (EMPHASIS ADDED)). A NEGLIGENT MISREPRESENTATION CAUSE OF ACTION CANNOT BE BASED ON A PROMISE REGARDING FUTURE CONDUCT. IT MUST BE MADE WITH RESPECT TO "A PAST OR EXISTING MATERIAL FACT." (TARMANN V. STATE FARM MUT. AUTO. INS. CO. (1991) 2 CAL.APP.4TH 152, 158.) MS. AKUTAGAWA DOES NOT MEANINGFULLY DISTINGUISH TARMANN. MS. AKUTAGAWA PLEADS HER TWO FRAUD CLAIMS WITH THE REQUISITE SPECIFICITY. HARLOW V. AMERICAN EQUITABLE INS. CO. (1927) 87 CAL.APP. 28 DOES NOT MANDATE DISMISSAL OF THE FRAUD CLAIMS. IN HARLOW IT WAS CLEAR THAT THE PARTIES HAD AN INSURANCE CONTRACT AND THE INSURED PLAINTIFF FAILED TO PERFORM BY GIVING TIMELY NOTICE AND PROOF OF LOSS. THE COURT DISMISSED THE FRAUD CLAIM ON DEMURRER BECAUSE EVEN IF THE INSURER HAD A FRAUDULENT INTENT TO NOT PERFORM THE INSURANCE POLICY, IT DID NOT MATTER BECAUSE THE INSURED WAS NOT PUT INTO A WORSE POSITION. IN THIS CASE, THE ALLEGED FRAUD PUT MS. AKUTAGAWA IN A WORSE POSITION BECAUSE SHE DID NOT RECEIVE THE SETTLEMENT FUNDS AND WAS INDUCED INTO WAIVING THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS WITH RESPECT TO HER WRONGFUL DEATH CLAIM. ORDER SIGNED. (SEE ORDER FOR FULL DETAILS) JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN, CLERK: JACQUELINE ALAMEDA, MATTER NOT REPORTED. =(302/HEK)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT ROBERT PENA'S MOTION TO STRIKE THE REQUESTS FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES IN THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFF JERILYNNE AKUTAGAWA IS DENIED. THE FRAUD CAUSES OF ACTION SUPPORT THE REQUESTS FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES. ORDER SIGNED. (SEE ORDER FOR FULL DETAILS) JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN, CLERK: JACQUELINE ALAMEDA, MATTER NOT REPORTED. =(302/HEK)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-06-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Joseph L. Schatz, Esq., 415.677.9151, appearing for the Plaintiff;
No appearance by any other party or counsel.

DEFENDANTS MARKEL AMERICAN INSURANCE COMPANY AND MARKEL INSURANCE COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO JERILYNNE AKUTAGAWA'S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

There being no other appearance, the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Defendants Markel American Insurance Company and Markel Insurance Company's demurrer to the second, third and fifth causes of action in the first amended complaint filed by plaintiff Jerilynne Akutagawa is sustained without leave to amend as to the second cause of action for negligent misrepresentation and overruled as to the third and fifth causes of action for fraud. Ms. Akutagawa alleges that defendants negligently misrepresented that they would pay the $125,000. (FAC par. 36 (emphasis added)). A negligent misrepresentation cause of action cannot be based on a promise regarding future conduct. It must be made with respect to "a past or existing material fact." (Tarmann v. State Farm Mut. Auto. Ins. Co. (1991) 2 Cal.App.4th 152, 158.) Ms. Akutagawa does not meaningfully distinguish Tarmann. Ms. Akutagawa pleads her two fraud claims with the requisite specificity. Harlow v. American Equitable Ins. Co. (1927) 87 Cal.App. 28 does not mandate dismissal of the fraud claims. In Harlow it was clear that the parties had an insurance contract and the insured plaintiff failed to perform by giving timely notice and proof of loss. The court dismissed the fraud claim on demurrer because even if the insurer had a fraudulent intent to not perform the insurance policy, it did not matter because the insured was not put into a worse position. In this case, the alleged fraud put Ms. Akutagawa in a worse position because she did not receive the settlement funds and was induced into waiving the statute of limitations with respect to her wrongful death claim. Order signed. (see order for full details)

Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda, Matter Not Reported. =(302/HEK)

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-06-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Joseph L. Schatz, Esq., 415.677.9151, appearing for the Plaintiff;
No appearance by any other party or counsel.

DEFENDANTS MARKEL AMERICAN INSURANCE COMPANY AND MARKEL INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF JERILYNN AKUTAGAWA'S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

There being no other appearance, the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Defendants Markel American Insurance Company and Markel Insurance Company's motion to strike the requests for punitive damages in the first amended complaint filed by plaintiff Jerilynne Akutagawa is denied. The fraud causes of action support the requests for punitive damages. Order signed. (see order for full details)

Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda, Matter Not Reported. =(302/HEK)

Auto. Ins. Co. (1991) 2 Cal.App.4th 152, 158.) Ms. Akutagawa does not meaningfully distinguish Tarmann. Ms. Akutagawa pleads her two fraud claims with the requisite specificity. Harlow v. American Equitable Ins. Co. (1927) 87 Cal.App. 28 does not mandate dismissal of the fraud claims. The court dismissed the fraud claim on demurrer because even if the insurer had a fraudulent intent to not perform the insurance policy, it did not matter because the insured was not put into a worse position. In this case, the alleged fraud put Ms. Akutagawa in a worse position because she did not receive the settlement funds and was induced into waiving the statute of limitations with respect to her wrongful death claim.
